topic.what: Japanese Society of Gastroenterology (JSGE) clinical practice guidelines for functional dyspepsia, formally recommending Rikkunshito as a treatment option supported by systematic evidence review.
topic.how: Systematic evidence grading using GRADE methodology applied to functional dyspepsia treatment literature; expert panel consensus for recommendation statements including Rikkunshito (TJ-43) based on available RCT and cohort data.
topic.why_matters: Official guideline endorsement by JSGE legitimizes Rikkunshito as a first-line or second-line treatment option in clinical practice, directly influencing prescribing behavior across Japanese gastroenterology and establishing a model for evidence-based integration of Kampo into mainstream medicine.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Formal strength-of-recommendation and evidence grade assigned to Rikkunshito for functional dyspepsia by JSGE
characteristics.value: Positive recommendation included in official guidelines; doi:10.1007/s00535-021-01843-7
characteristics.vs_baseline: Advancement from off-label use to guideline-endorsed treatment option
characteristics.context: Represents the highest level of clinical endorsement available in the Japanese healthcare system

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number and quality of studies underpinning the Rikkunshito recommendation
characteristics.value: Multiple RCTs and systematic reviews cited; moderate evidence grade
characteristics.vs_baseline: Broader evidence base than prior guideline versions
characteristics.evidence: Systematic evidence review in JSGE guideline; pmid:35061057
characteristics.context: Evidence grade reflects available trial quality; strengthened by ghrelin mechanistic data

characteristics.dimension: Scope of Kampo integration in guidelines
characteristics.what_it_measures: Extent to which Kampo medicines are incorporated alongside conventional pharmacotherapy options
characteristics.value: Rikkunshito explicitly named alongside proton pump inhibitors and prokinetics
characteristics.vs_baseline: Greater Kampo integration vs. prior guideline editions
characteristics.context: Signals mainstream acceptance of evidence-based Kampo in gastroenterology

good_for: Healthcare policy makers evaluating Kampo reimbursement
Researchers documenting evidence-based integration of traditional medicine
International clinicians seeking precedent for Kampo guideline inclusion
Guideline developers in other East Asian countries considering similar frameworks
not_for: Direct patient care outside Japanese clinical context without adaptation
Countries where Rikkunshito preparations are not approved or standardized
Mechanistic research (guideline document, not primary research)
requires: Access to standardized Rikkunshito preparations approved by Japanese regulatory authorities
Clinical infrastructure supporting Kampo prescription and monitoring
Practitioner training in functional dyspepsia diagnosis per Rome criteria

novelty: First Japanese national gastroenterology guideline to formally grade and recommend a Kampo formula using GRADE methodology
Establishes a replicable template for evidence-based Kampo integration in other disease-area guidelines
Positions Japan as international leader in evidence-graded traditional medicine integration
open_problems: Recommendation strength limited by available trial quality; upgrade contingent on larger multicenter RCTs
Guideline applicability outside Japan requires cultural and regulatory adaptation
Long-term safety monitoring infrastructure for Kampo at population scale not fully established
Interaction between Rikkunshito and conventional FD treatments (PPIs, prokinetics) not addressed in guideline
